assertions.expectation: Checks for deprecated API usage that may have changed behavior in 1.30
assertions.reason: The response mentions checking for deprecated API usage in the context of the 1.31 to 1.32 upgrade, but it does not mention version 1.30 or API changes specific to it.

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking admission webhooks — they may be rejecting or slowing down requests on the new version
assertions.reason: The response has a dedicated section for Admission Webhooks, noting they intercept API requests and can cause massive latency or block pod creation if they are struggling on the new 1.32 nodes.

assertions.expectation: Advises checking if resource requests/limits need adjusting for any system components that changed
assertions.reason: The response does not suggest checking or adjusting resource requests/limits for system components.

assertions.expectation: Suggests examining kube-system pods for any that are crashlooping or restarting
assertions.reason: The response explicitly advises to 'Look for CrashLoopBackOff in the kube-system namespace' and provides the command 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system'.

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to check API server metrics and latency (kubectl get --raw /metrics)
assertions.reason: The response does not include the 'kubectl get --raw /metrics' command or any other commands specific to querying raw API server metrics.

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking if NetworkPolicy or service mesh components need updating for the new version
assertions.reason: While the response mentions service mesh components (Istio, Linkerd) causing webhook timeouts, it does not mention NetworkPolicy, nor does it explicitly recommend updating these components for the new version.

assertions.expectation: Advises reviewing pod events for scheduling delays or readiness probe failures
assertions.reason: The response advises checking pod events for probe failures, explicitly stating 'check their events for probe failures: kubectl describe pod <POD_NAME> -n <NAMESPACE> | grep -i probe'.

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if HPA or VPA are behaving differently on the new version
assertions.reason: The response does not mention the Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA) or Vertical Pod Autoscaler (VPA).
